name: dual_narrative_crm_hybrid_empty_halves
description:
Pins the THIRD arm of the CRM responsibility cross-check inside validate_dual_narratives (engine/validator.py:704-712) -- the `resp == 'hybrid' and not onprem and not cloud` branch. Sibling pair with dual_narrative_crm_customer_mismatch.json (which pins arm #1: customer + populated cloud at validator.py:686-694). Arm #2 (provider/inherited + populated on-prem at validator.py:695-703) requires dual-scope CRM gymnastics to bypass the assessor.py:748-751 short-circuit (specified=[provider,customer] avoids _CRM_SHORT_CIRCUIT_SET); deferred to a follow-up slice.

Why this arm matters specifically -- hybrid responsibility is the ONLY CRM value where the cross-check fires on the ABSENCE of content rather than its presence. Arms #1 and #2 catch over-population (LLM filled a half that shouldn't exist); arm #3 catches under-population (LLM forgot the whole point of the split). Both shapes of error need pinning because the failure mode is asymmetric: an LLM that knows 'hybrid means split across customer/provider' but doesn't have artifact-grounded language for either half will sometimes return both halves empty rather than guess -- and without this branch firing, the row would land as Compliant with a structurally meaningless dual-narrative payload that the column-Q-split UI renderer would render as two empty bullet sections.

Mechanism trace:
  - crm_entries supplies a single CRM row with responsibility='hybrid', responsibility_onprem=null, control_id='sc-7' (OSCAL canonical lowercase to match _ccis_to_oscal_control_id output).
  - Assessor._lookup_crm returns the entry. specified=['hybrid']. assessor.py:748-751 short-circuit check: 'hybrid' is NOT in _CRM_SHORT_CIRCUIT_SET={'provider','inherited','not_applicable'} -> short-circuit does NOT fire. Good -- LLM path runs.
  - assessor.py:762 hybrid block check: `'hybrid' in specified` -> True -> _render_hybrid_block prepends responsibility-split text to tagged_evidence. This is a confounder ONLY if the prepended block bleeds into validator phrase tables -- verified below it does not.
  - LLM proposes status=Compliant, valid column Q narrative, AND BOTH dual halves empty string (narrative_on_prem='', narrative_cloud='').
  - Main validate() pass on column Q:
    - _AFFIRMING_PHRASES hits: 'examined ' (validator.py:57), 'confirmed via' (line 44), 'documented in' (line 54). classes_hit=1 -> _classify_single returns COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING.
    - _expected_status_for_class(COMPLIANCE_AFFIRMING) -> COMPLIANT; proposed Compliant matches -> no STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH.
    - No 'inherited from' -> MISSING_INHERITANCE_MARKER skipped (validator.py:508 only fires when the phrase is present).
    - Jaccard vs procedures stays below _OVERLAP_THRESHOLD=0.70 (procedures is generic boundary-protection text; column Q cites specific document number + section + AO).
    - No SV-#####r#_rule -> UNCORROBORATED_STIG_PASS skipped.
    - No future-tense -> FUTURE_TENSE_COMPLIANCE skipped.
    - USD00099999 appears in tagged_evidence -> UNSUPPORTED_DOC_CITATION skipped.
    - Column Q narrative does NOT contain _HYBRID_SPLIT_RE markers ('On-prem:'/'Cloud:'/'## responsibility_split') so classify_narrative takes the single-block path; the dual halves are isolated in narrative_on_prem / narrative_cloud (UI-fidelity fields), not embedded in column Q.
  - crm_resp_for_check resolution at assessor.py:1296-1303: cloud_r2='hybrid', onprem_r2=None -> else branch: `crm_resp_for_check = 'hybrid' or None = 'hybrid'`.
  - validate_dual_narratives(narrative_on_prem='', narrative_cloud='', crm_responsibility='hybrid'):
    - onprem = ''.strip() = ''. cloud = ''.strip() = ''. Both falsy.
    - Phrase-table leak at validator.py:665: `if onprem and _has_any(...)` -> '' is falsy -> branch skipped. Safe -- no DUAL_NARRATIVE_MISLABEL leak.
    - Phrase-table leak at validator.py:673: `if cloud and _has_any(...)` -> '' is falsy -> branch skipped. Safe.
    - CRM cross-check at validator.py:686: `resp == 'customer'` -> False (resp='hybrid'). Skipped.
    - CRM cross-check at validator.py:695: `resp in ('provider', 'inherited')` -> False. Skipped.
    - CRM cross-check at validator.py:704: `resp == 'hybrid'` -> True. `not onprem and not cloud` -> True and True -> True. notes.append + flagged.append(DUAL_NARRATIVE_MISLABEL). 
  - dual_result.notes / flagged carry the single flag back to the orchestrator. The dual-narrative result is ADVISORY -- column Q already passed validate() so the verdict stays Compliant and source='llm', llm_calls=1, needs_review=False. Only the dual_narrative_flag is stamped for the column-Q-split UI renderer to surface the under-populated halves for human review.

Load-bearing isolation -- nothing else can fire and confuse the assertion:
  - Both halves are EMPTY string (not None) to exercise the exact `not onprem and not cloud` predicate at validator.py:705. If either were populated with even whitespace ('  '), the `.strip()` at validator.py:657-658 would normalize to '' so the predicate still fires, but the empty-string literal in the case JSON is the most direct expression of the failure mode.
  - responsibility_onprem is null -- single-scope hybrid keeps specified=['hybrid']. A dual-scope CRM row would change crm_resp_for_check resolution at assessor.py:1300 (`cloud_r2 and onprem_r2 and cloud_r2 != onprem_r2 -> 'hybrid'`) and potentially flip needs_hybrid_block calculation. Single-scope is the cleanest pin.
  - Column Q is independently valid -- the dual-narrative cross-check is ADVISORY-ONLY in the validator (DualNarrativeResult.notes / flagged are appended; no hard rejection). The verdict path runs through main validate() which doesn't see the dual halves at all. Without a valid column Q, the verdict would route through STATUS_NARRATIVE_MISMATCH and retry-exhaust before ever reaching validate_dual_narratives.
  - crm_entries.narrative is short and free of any _PROVIDER_ONLY_PHRASES / _ONPREM_ONLY_PHRASES tokens. Even though the hybrid prepend block (assessor.py:768) would inject that text into tagged_evidence (not into narrative_on_prem / narrative_cloud), being conservative about phrase content ensures no spurious confounders.
  - Procedures (col K) does NOT name a CSP -> Rule 8b doesn't pre-empt the LLM. Procedures does not contain 'auto-compliant per' or Rule 8a markers. SC-7 / CCI-001097 is not in the SDA Controls mapping (Rule 8c skipped).
  - tagged_evidence is non-empty so Step 1.65 no-evidence short-circuit at assessor.py:833 does NOT fire.
  - dual_narrative_flags_contains assertion expects exactly ['dual_narrative_mislabel'] -- a regression that adds extra flags or returns empty would fail loudly.

Mutation differentiation -- neutralizing the hybrid arm at validator.py:704-712 (e.g. `elif False and resp == 'hybrid':`):
  - dual_result.flagged would not contain DUAL_NARRATIVE_MISLABEL.
  - Runner's dual_narrative_flags_contains assertion fails: expected 'dual_narrative_mislabel', got [].
  - Arms #1 (dual_narrative_crm_customer_mismatch) and leak-detector cases (dual_narrative_leak_advisory, dual_narrative_leak_cloud_onprem_terms) STILL pass because they exercise different branches. Hybrid-empty regression caught ONLY by this case.

Alternative mutation -- swapping `not onprem and not cloud` for `not onprem or not cloud` at validator.py:705:
  - The OR is strictly more lenient (fires when EITHER half is empty); both-empty still matches both predicates. THIS case would still pass.
  - Cross-case regression: a future case with ONE populated and ONE empty half would erroneously fire under the OR but not under the AND. Cross-case coverage by design -- pinning this case + adding a 'one populated, one empty' counter-case would close the predicate completely. Counter-case deferred to a follow-up slice (small, mechanical follow-on).

Alternative mutation -- flipping the empty-check direction (`if onprem or cloud:` instead of `if not onprem and not cloud:`):
  - Predicate inverts -- branch fires when at least one half is populated; both-empty case (THIS case) does NOT fire.
  - dual_result.flagged is empty -> assertion fails -> mutation caught.

Substrate: SC-7 (Boundary Protection) / CCI-001097 (boundary protection at external interfaces) is the canonical hybrid-responsibility control in FedRAMP-Moderate/High systems -- customer owns local boundary devices, provider owns cloud egress. An LLM that retrieves both-side artifacts but can't articulate which half owns the customer-managed VPC peering vs. the provider-managed Transit Gateway might legitimately return a valid column Q (the overall control is implemented) while leaving the dual halves empty rather than guess scope attribution. That's exactly the realistic mode this arm catches.

ccis_row.guidance: The information system monitors and controls communications at the external boundary of the system.
ccis_row.procedures: Examine the boundary protection mechanisms and confirm communications are monitored and controlled at external interfaces.

tagged_evidence:
## Tagged evidence
- USD00099999 Boundary Protection Plan Rev C — documents external interface monitoring and AO-approved control point inventory.

crm_entries.narrative: Customer owns boundary protection for the local enclave; provider owns boundary protection for cloud egress. Joint operational responsibility documented in the shared responsibility matrix.

llm_stub_proposals.narrative: Examined the program boundary protection documentation USD00099999; confirmed via section 5 that external interface monitoring is documented in the AO-approved control point inventory.
